title: Fix 5 standing failures that are stale tests (#3, #4, #5, #8, #14 — triage bucket A)
intent: Batch A of the api-verify standing-failures triage. Five residual reds are STALE TESTS — the product moved and the tests didn't. #3/#4/#5 the IBG solve-from-landmarks endpoint gained Depends(get_current_principal) in IBG-2B, so unauthenticated TestClient POSTs now 401; #8 a permissive-gate test approves before attempting bypass, so the bypass counter never increments; #14 a lifecycle fixture used a synthetic translator_id that 7C's registry validation rejects. Each was re-grounded against current code before fixing. Test-only; no production code touched, no behavior change.

diff_articulation.what_changed: (#3/#4/#5) test_morphology_spine_e2e.py: the three failing tests POST to /api/body/solve-from-landmarks (a paid-tier endpoint that gained Depends(get_current_principal) in IBG-2B) without auth, so TestClient got 401. Added a module-level _AUTH_HEADERS = {'x-user-role':'operator','x-user-id':'test_user_123'} (the same legacy header-auth convention the passing test_body_solver_integration.py uses) and passed headers=_AUTH_HEADERS to the three solve POSTs. #5 additionally had NO status guard before ibg_data['outline_points'] (so the 401 body caused KeyError, not a clear failure); added the assert solve_response.status_code == 200 guard the sibling tests already have. (#8) test_ibg_intake_gate.py::test_permissive_gate_tolerates_bypass_attempts: reordered so the two machine bypass attempts (set_review_required(False,'system:bot')) run BEFORE the human approval. set_review_required only increments _bypass_attempt_count when _review_required is still True; approving first cleared the flag, so the attempts no-op'd and the counter stayed 0 (REVIEW_BYPASS_DETECTED never fired). Attempting pre-approval makes the counter reach 2, so the default gate rejects and the permissive gate (allow=2) accepts. (#14) test_operation_capability_registry.py: the shared _create_lifecycle_request helper hardcoded translator_id='test_translator', which 7C validate_translator_registry rejects (not in TRANSLATOR_CAPABILITY_REGISTRY) -> translator stage RED. Changed to 'dxf_r12' (category=translator, output_class=dxf, execution_state=validation_only not disabled, supported_operations include nut_slot/drilling) so test_registered_operation_works gets green/yellow. REVIEW HARDENING (2026-07-02, addressing a risk review of this PR): (a) test_morphology_spine_e2e.py — promoted the ad-hoc module-level _AUTH_HEADERS constant to a documented `auth_headers` pytest fixture (matching test_body_solver_integration.py's `auth_header`), threaded through the three test signatures and call sites; kept headers explicit at each call (not baked into an authenticated client) so a future auth-model migration surfaces loudly instead of passing silently. (b) test_operation_capability_registry.py — replaced the hardcoded translator_id='dxf_r12' with _registered_translator_id_for(operation), which resolves from the capability registry (list_translators_for_operation, else any registry-valid DXF translator via list_translators_by_output_class + validate_translator_registry); resilient to rename/disable of any single translator. (c) test_ibg_intake_gate.py — added explicit assertions on the intermediate/final review state (bypass_attempt_count==2 and review_required True before approval / False after) so failures pinpoint the state transition rather than only surfacing via gate-rejection results. Verified free-tier /api/body/solve-from-dxf has no get_current_principal, so the untouched real-artifact spine tests correctly need no auth headers.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: All five hypotheses were re-verified against current code (the earlier #1/#2 diagnosis had proven wrong, so no triage row was trusted). #3/#4/#5 share one root cause (the IBG-2B auth gate, commit 70a0d3ee) and the exact header convention was copied from a PASSING test, not guessed. #8's reorder was validated against the set_review_required increment guard (`if self._review_required and not required`). #14's replacement id was confirmed present and valid in TRANSLATOR_CAPABILITY_REGISTRY, and no sibling test depends on 'test_translator' being rejected (test_unregistered_operation_returns_red is red because of the OPERATION, not the translator). All test-only; zero product behavior change.

commands_run: services/api venv (py3.11.9): pytest tests/cam/test_operation_capability_registry.py tests/test_ibg_intake_gate.py tests/mrp_spine_verification/test_morphology_spine_e2e.py -> 58 passed, 1 skipped in 57s (post review-hardening: auth_headers fixture, registry-driven translator id, explicit bypass-state asserts; the 1 skip is a real-artifact spine test with no DXF present)

registry lookup confirmed: list_translators_for_operation('nut_slot'/'drilling') -> dxf_r12/dxf_r2000, both pass validate_translator_registry (category=translator, output=dxf, not disabled); unregistered_op falls back to a valid DXF translator so the RED still comes from the operation, not the translator id

auth scoping confirmed: body_solver_router.py — solve-from-landmarks + landmark PUT use Depends(get_current_principal); solve-from-dxf is free-tier (no principal), so the untouched melody_maker/cuatro tests need no headers
note: full api-verify is an Ubuntu-CI witness (fastapi 0.137 pin); local venv is fastapi 0.121.1

files.intent: #3/#4/#5: authenticate the three solve-from-landmarks POSTs via a shared documented `auth_headers` fixture (was ad-hoc _AUTH_HEADERS const); #5's 200 guard; headers kept explicit at call sites
#8: attempt bypasses before approval so the counter increments; assert bypass_attempt_count/review_required intermediate+final state directly
#14: resolve a registry-valid translator id via _registered_translator_id_for(operation) (was hardcoded dxf_r12, before that synthetic test_translator) — resilient to registry churn
